bobReleaseAndDlcAccess.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Release Date and DLC Access
bobReleaseAndDlcAccess.intro: Bob is the second additional playable character in TEKKEN 8 Season 3. Season Pass 3 holders receive 5-day early access before standalone availability opens to all players. North America and Europe list different calendar dates because store timing is handled by region.

rows.platformStore: PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam
rows.keyNotes: Bob joins Season 3 after Kunimitsu, with Roger Jr. and Yujiro Hanma also listed for the pass.

rows.platformStore: PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam
rows.keyNotes: Individual DLCs are sold separately, so pass owners avoid duplicate purchases.

rows.platformStore: PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam
rows.keyNotes: The European announcement lists Bob as the second playable character of Season 3.

rows.platformStore: PS5, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam
rows.keyNotes: The European date follows the regional store calendar for all-player access.

rows.accessWindow: Bob listed for Jul-Sep 2026 in Season 3 Pass content
rows.purchaseMethod: TEKKEN 8 Season 3 Pass add-on for the Steam version
rows.keyNotes: Requires the TEKKEN 8 base game on Steam. The pass includes four additional playable characters, one additional battle stage, the Aurora Outfit Pack, and 5-day early access for future DLC.

rows.accessWindow: Bob listed for Jul-Sep 2026 in Season 3 Pass content
rows.purchaseMethod: TEKKEN 8 Season 3 Pass add-on for PS5
rows.keyNotes: The PS5 store page lists Kunimitsu, Bob, Roger Jr., Yujiro Hanma, SUBSEA LOCKDOWN, the Aurora Outfit Pack, and 5-day early access for future DLC.

rows.accessWindow: Bob listed for Jul-Sep 2026 in Season 3 Pass content
rows.purchaseMethod: TEKKEN 8 Season 3 Pass add-on for Xbox Series X|S
rows.keyNotes: The Xbox listing marks the pass as content that requires TEKKEN 8, sold separately.

bobTrailerBreakdown.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Gameplay Trailer Breakdown
bobTrailerBreakdown.intro: The official Bob gameplay reveal trailer brings back the TEKKEN 6 fighter as the Onigiri Savior. It focuses on his speed-and-weight identity, Season 3 role, and new TEKKEN 8 presentation, covering what was shown, Bob's story setup, his Season 3 role, and the combat ideas already confirmed by official sources.

cards.label: Reveal Context
cards.headline: Shown during EVO 2026 in Las Vegas
cards.body: Bandai Namco revealed Bob's TEKKEN 8 gameplay trailer during EVO 2026, where he was presented as the second DLC character for Season 3.

cards.label: Official Trailer
cards.headline: TEKKEN 8 - Bob Gameplay Reveal Trailer
cards.body: The official media page lists the Bob Gameplay Reveal Trailer under Fighter Reveals, and the YouTube trailer promotes his Speed & Weight identity with an August 19, 2026 early-access date.

cards.label: Returning Identity
cards.headline: Speed, Weight, and Power
cards.body: Bob returns with the same core idea that defined him in earlier TEKKEN games: a large martial artist with surprising agility, strong power, and a self-proclaimed perfect body.

cards.label: Story Setup
cards.headline: From burger chain success to Onigiri Savior
cards.body: After recovering from injuries linked to Bryan Fury, Bob builds a successful burger chain, launches Bob's Onigiri, and later supports people during G Corp's invasion of New York.

cards.label: Season 3 Role
cards.headline: Second Season 3 playable character
cards.body: Bob joins Kunimitsu in TEKKEN 8 Season 3, with Roger Jr. and Yujiro Hanma also listed as upcoming Season 3 Pass characters.

cards.label: Combat Preview
cards.headline: Large-frame pressure with agile movement
cards.body: Official commentary describes Bob as a flexible all-rounder who switches between powerful attacks and quick options, especially in close- to mid-range grounded combat.

cards.label: Confirmed Tools
cards.headline: Giga Jacker, Perfect Body, United Step, and Spinner Ball
cards.body: The development notes highlight Giga Jacker as a fast-starting Power Crush, Perfect Body as a new Attack Reversal, and returning stance tools including United Step and Spinner Ball.

cards.label: Heat Highlight
cards.headline: Just-frame Heat Smash
cards.body: TEKKEN 8 Bob's Heat Smash includes a just-frame input: successful timing increases damage and triggers an alternate animation.

bobBeginnerGuide.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Beginner Guide
bobBeginnerGuide.intro: Bob is built around Speed & Weight, Freestyle Karate, and a flexible all-rounder game plan. This route is for new Bob players and returning TEKKEN players who need a clean starting plan focused on his close- to mid-range tools, agile heavy-body style, Heat and Rage basics, throws, Special Style, and stance-based offense.

steps.title: Understand Bob's identity
steps.goal: Treat Bob as a speed-and-weight all-rounder, not a slow big-body character.
details: Country of origin: United States.
Fighting style: Freestyle Karate.
Core theme: Speed, Weight, and Power.
Beginner focus: use quick movement and heavy attacks together instead of relying on size alone.

steps.title: Play close to mid range
steps.goal: Build Bob around grounded pressure where his confirmed strengths are strongest.
details: Official notes describe Bob as effective in close- to mid-range engagements.
Use pressure to restrict the opponent's options and create follow-up chances.
Expect his best flow to come from switching between fast attacks, strong hits, and stance pressure.

steps.title: Learn the universal controls first
steps.goal: Get comfortable with TEKKEN 8's shared systems before memorizing Bob-specific commands.
details: Basic attack buttons cover left punch, right punch, left kick, and right kick.
Throws use the standard throw inputs shown in the official starter guide.
Special Style can be toggled for simpler control while learning spacing and timing.

steps.title: Use Heat to stay aggressive
steps.goal: Activate Heat once per round to support offense and pressure.
details: Heat Burst activates Heat with a smashing attack.
Heat Engagers activate Heat on hit and give strong advantage as the character rushes forward.
Heat Smash consumes the Heat gauge for a powerful attack.
Heat Dash can extend pressure after a Heat Engager.

steps.title: Respect Rage and recoverable health
steps.goal: Use low-health situations and recoverable gauge rules to avoid panic decisions.
details: Rage activates automatically when health drops below a set amount.
Rage increases attack power and enables Rage Art.
Recoverable health can be regained by attacking or forcing the opponent to block.

steps.title: Practice Bob's stance flow
steps.goal: Start with United Step and Spinner Ball as Bob's key stance-pressure ideas.
details: United Step returns with attacks such as Langue Washer and Cutting Coppa.
Spinner Ball returns with expanded options in TEKKEN 8.
Spinner Ball can be used in aerial combos and includes new pressure tools tied to TEKKEN 8's systems.

bobMoveListAndStances.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Move List and Stances
bobMoveListAndStances.intro: Bob's official TEKKEN 8 kit confirms Freestyle Karate, United Step, Spinner Ball, Power Crush, Attack Reversal, Heat pressure, and several named attacks. This module organizes confirmed Bob mechanics from official character pages and development commentary, giving users real move names and functions now while keeping full input notation assigned to the launch movelist update.

sections.summary: Bob is a United States fighter using Freestyle Karate with a Speed & Weight identity.

entries.name: Freestyle Karate
entries.confirmedUse: Bob combines large-frame power with agile movement and flexible offense.

entries.name: Speed & Weight
entries.confirmedUse: His core concept balances speed, weight, and power as the basis of his martial arts style.

sections.summary: Bob is described as a grounded close- to mid-range all-rounder.

entries.name: Close- to Mid-Range Pressure
entries.confirmedUse: Bob uses a wide range of grounded tools to pressure opponents and adapt between powerful and quick attacks.

entries.name: All-Rounder Response Tools
entries.confirmedUse: Official development notes describe him as strong at responding to the opponent's actions, while his punishes demand practice to execute.

sections.summary: Bob has confirmed defensive-response options for reversing momentum.

entries.name: Giga Jacker
entries.confirmedUse: A fast-starting Power Crush option used to turn the tables during pressure.

entries.name: Perfect Body
entries.confirmedUse: A new Attack Reversal in TEKKEN 8 and one of the move names highlighted by the development team.

sections.summary: United Step returns as one of Bob's key stance-pressure routes.

entries.name: United Step
entries.confirmedUse: A stance route tied to Bob's signature pressure and follow-up attacks.

entries.name: Langue Washer
entries.confirmedUse: A returning signature attack from United Step that helps apply constant pressure.

entries.name: Cutting Coppa
entries.confirmedUse: A returning signature attack from United Step that restricts the opponent's options and creates high-damage follow-up chances.

sections.summary: Spinner Ball returns with expanded TEKKEN 8 options.

entries.name: Spinner Ball
entries.confirmedUse: A stance that gains new options in TEKKEN 8, including a Heat Engager and attacks that help maintain pressure even when blocked.

entries.name: Roulette Cutter
entries.confirmedUse: Shown in the official development breakdown as part of Bob's expanded TEKKEN 8 move set.

entries.name: Low Attack into Spinner Ball
entries.confirmedUse: Bob has a unique move that shifts into Spinner Ball from a low attack, giving him another way to open the opponent's defense.

sections.summary: Bob's TEKKEN 8 system tools include Heat pressure and a timing-based Heat Smash highlight.

entries.name: Heat Smash
entries.confirmedUse: Bob's Heat Smash features a just-frame input; correct timing increases damage and triggers an alternate animation.

entries.name: Spinner Ball Heat Engager
entries.confirmedUse: Spinner Ball has a new Heat Engager option in TEKKEN 8.

entries.name: Rage Art
entries.confirmedUse: Bob's Rage Art is highlighted for its dynamic, comical presentation.

sections.summary: Bob's TEKKEN 8 move names also reflect his Onigiri Savior story.

entries.name: Onigiri Bomber
entries.confirmedUse: A new move name highlighted by the development team and tied to Bob's TEKKEN 8 story theme.

sections.summary: The full command notation is assigned to the launch movelist update section.

entries.name: Full command inputs
entries.confirmedUse: This slot is prepared for Bob's exact directional and button inputs from the TEKKEN 8 launch movelist.

bobCombos.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Combos
bobCombos.intro: Build Bob routes around his confirmed speed-and-weight toolkit, United Step pressure, Spinner Ball extensions, Heat Dash conversions, and aerial combo options. Bob returns as a Freestyle Karate all-rounder built around power, agile movement, and close- to mid-range control.

cards.title: Core Launch Route
cards.summary: Start with Bob's standard launcher practice, then route into stable aerial carry before spending Heat or chasing wall position.
details: Bob's official kit emphasizes powerful attacks backed by agile movement.
Use launcher routes to test how his large-frame power converts into consistent air combos.
Prioritize stable carry and clean enders before adding Heat or stance extensions.

cards.title: Spinner Ball Aerial Extension
cards.summary: Spinner Ball is the key confirmed stance for Bob's TEKKEN 8 combo routing because it can be incorporated into aerial combos.
details: Spinner Ball returns from earlier TEKKEN entries and receives expanded TEKKEN 8 options.
The stance supports aerial combo use, giving Bob flashier and stronger combo routes.
Roulette Cutter is shown as part of the expanded Spinner Ball toolkit.

cards.title: United Step Follow-up Route
cards.summary: United Step keeps Bob's offense active through returning tools such as Langue Washer and Cutting Coppa.
details: United Step gives Bob close- to mid-range offense that restricts the opponent's choices.
Langue Washer and Cutting Coppa return as signature United Step attacks.
These attacks create pressure and set up high-damage follow-up opportunities.

cards.title: Heat Dash Conversion
cards.summary: Use Heat Engager routes to convert into Heat Dash combo pressure, then route into air-combo behavior under Season 3 rules.
details: Heat Engager can activate Heat on hit and rush Bob toward the opponent with major advantage.
Heat Dash after a Heat Engager changes opponent behavior on hit and opens combo possibilities.
Season 3 adjusts Heat Dash combo starter behavior and combo scaling, so Bob routes should be practiced under current patch rules.

cards.title: Wall Carry and Wall Ender
cards.summary: Build wall routes around Bob's mobility, aerial carry, and pressure tools rather than forcing Heat Smash wall splat routes.
details: Bob's blend of power and speed supports route planning from midscreen to wall pressure.
Season 3 removes Heat Smash wall splat behavior across affected characters.
Use wall carry to set up grounded pressure, United Step checks, and Heat follow-up decisions.

cards.title: Low-to-Spinner Ball Route
cards.summary: Bob has a TEKKEN 8 low attack that shifts into Spinner Ball, giving him a direct way to open defense and continue offense.
details: The low-to-Spinner Ball transition expands Bob's offense from grounded situations.
During Heat, Bob gains additional follow-up attacks that strengthen grounded combat.
Use this route family to connect low-threat pressure with Spinner Ball follow-ups.

bobHeatSystemGuide.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Heat System Guide
bobHeatSystemGuide.intro: Use Bob's Heat around Spinner Ball, Heat Engagers, Heat Dash conversions, just-frame Heat Smash timing, and Season 3 balance rules. Bob's Heat plan strengthens the parts of his kit the official team highlighted most: grounded combat, close- to mid-range pressure, Spinner Ball offense, and follow-up attacks that help cover his harder punishment game.

steps.title: Enter Heat with a Clear Purpose
steps.action: Activate Heat through Heat Burst or a Heat Engager, then immediately choose between pressure, conversion, or Heat Smash cashout.
details: Heat is available once per round.
Heat Burst activates Heat while attacking and gives slight advantage on hit or guard.
Heat Engager activates Heat on hit and rushes the character forward with strong advantage.

steps.title: Use Spinner Ball Heat Engager Pressure
steps.action: Use the expanded Spinner Ball stance as Bob's Heat pressure hub.
details: Spinner Ball now includes a Heat Engager.
The stance has added attacks that keep Bob's offense active even when blocked.
This gives Bob a direct pressure route from stance movement into Heat advantage.

steps.title: Convert with Heat Dash
steps.action: After a Heat Engager, trigger Heat Dash to create combo routes on hit or pressure advantage on block.
details: Heat Dash after a landed Heat Engager changes opponent behavior and leads to combo possibilities.
Heat Dash after a blocked Heat Engager lets Bob rush in and gain advantage.
Heat Dash consumes the Heat state, so route choice matters before spending it.

steps.title: Spend Heat Smash for Damage and Timing Reward
steps.action: Use Bob's Heat Smash as the big Heat spend, with extra value from the just-frame input.
details: Heat Smash consumes all remaining Heat gauge and ends Heat.
Bob's Heat Smash has a just-frame input.
Correct timing increases damage and triggers an alternate animation.

steps.title: Apply Season 3 Heat Rules
steps.action: Route Bob's Heat pressure around the current Season 3 system changes.
details: Heat Smash wall splat behavior was removed for affected characters in Season 3.
Character-specific powered-up states now end when Heat ends.
Heat Dash combo starter behavior and Heat-related rewards were adjusted to reduce excessive outcomes.

steps.title: Pair Heat with Rage Art Awareness
steps.action: When Bob enters Rage, balance Heat decisions with Rage Art threat and recoverable-gauge removal.
details: Rage activates automatically at low health.
Rage Art can absorb attacks and counterattack.
A landed Rage Art erases the opponent's recoverable gauge.

bobTierListAndMetaRole.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Tier List and Meta Role
bobTierListAndMetaRole.intro: Bob profiles as a speed-and-weight all-rounder with stance pressure, Heat value, defensive turn-stealing, and official tournament availability after his standard release date. His meta role is built around flexible offense: powerful large-frame attacks, agile movement, grounded close- to mid-range control, United Step pressure, Spinner Ball Heat routes, and defensive tools such as Giga Jacker and Perfect Body.

tiers.label: Beginner Friendliness
tiers.summary: Bob's all-rounder design gives new players a clear game plan built around power, speed, and grounded pressure.
reasons: The official team describes Bob as a true all-rounder.
His offense can switch between heavy attacks and quick attacks depending on the situation.
His punishment game demands practice, so defense-to-punish drills remain important.

tiers.label: Pressure
tiers.summary: United Step, Langue Washer, Cutting Coppa, and expanded Spinner Ball options give Bob steady close- and mid-range pressure.
reasons: United Step returns as a core stance.
Langue Washer and Cutting Coppa restrict opponent choices and create high-damage follow-ups.
Spinner Ball adds block-pressure options and a Heat Engager.

tiers.label: Mobility
tiers.summary: Bob keeps his signature contrast: large-frame power paired with movement that is unusually agile for his size.
reasons: Official material highlights Bob's blend of speed, weight, and power.
His agile movement supports close- to mid-range engagements.
Spinner Ball and United Step give him stance-based movement layers.

tiers.label: Defense
tiers.summary: Giga Jacker and Perfect Body give Bob strong ways to turn the table when the opponent overextends.
reasons: Giga Jacker is a fast-starting Power Crush.
Perfect Body is a new Attack Reversal.
His response tools are strong, while his punishment execution demands practice.

tiers.label: Heat Value
tiers.summary: Bob's Heat improves grounded combat, adds follow-up attacks, and gives extra threat through Spinner Ball and just-frame Heat Smash.
reasons: During Heat, Bob gains additional tools that help cover his punishment demands.
Bob's Heat Smash has a just-frame timing reward.
Season 3 Heat changes reduce excessive wall and Heat Dash outcomes across the system.

tiers.label: Tournament Role
tiers.summary: Bob follows Tekken World Tour DLC rules: playable from his official release date, not during the early-access window.
reasons: TWT 2026 uses TEKKEN 8 with official match settings on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, or Steam.
DLC characters become tournament-legal on the official release date, with time zone differences handled by event location.
Early-access windows are not legal for tournament use.

bobStoryAndCostumes.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob Story and Costumes
bobStoryAndCostumes.intro: Bob returns as the Onigiri Savior: a Freestyle Karate martial artist, fast-food success story, emergency investigator, and Kazuya opponent with a specific rice-ball agenda. His TEKKEN 8 story turns the pursuit of the perfect body into a mix of martial arts, food relief, public heroism, and legal action against Kazuya Mishima, while his costumes shift from relaxed training wear to formal investigator attire.

cards.title: Character Profile
cards.summary: Bob is a United States fighter who uses Freestyle Karate and defines the perfect body through speed, weight, and power.
facts: Country of origin: United States.
Fighting style: Freestyle Karate.
Character theme: Speed & Weight.

cards.title: From Hospital Food to Burger Empire
cards.summary: After being hospitalized from the Bryan Fury incident, Bob rejects bland recovery meals and creates his own hamburger recipe.
facts: Bob fought Bryan Fury while trying to prevent casualties.
A road collapse left Bob hospitalized.
After release, he launched a successful burger chain.

cards.title: Bob's Onigiri
cards.summary: Bob's Onigiri becomes a signature menu item and sparks a major onigiri craze across the United States.
facts: The item builds on Bob's fast-food success.
The onigiri theme becomes central to his TEKKEN 8 identity.
The official story connects onigiri with Bob's support for ordinary people.

cards.title: The Onigiri Savior
cards.summary: When G Corp targets New York, Bob uses his food network to provide free hamburgers and onigiri to people in need.
facts: G Corp's invasion reaches New York under Kazuya Mishima.
Bob begins food relief work during the crisis.
His public support earns him the nickname Onigiri Savior.

cards.title: Kazuya Conflict
cards.summary: Bob accepts an emergency investigator role from the Manhattan District Attorney's Office and sets out to bring Kazuya to justice.
facts: The Manhattan District Attorney's Office recognizes Bob's justice-driven action.
Bob accepts the investigator role despite the sudden responsibility.
His TEKKEN 8 conflict centers on stopping Kazuya Mishima.

cards.title: Onigiri Wordplay
cards.summary: Bob's special interaction with Kazuya uses Japanese wordplay, connecting onigiri with slaying an oni, or Devil.
facts: The joke comes from a viral in-story social media post.
Bob adopts the phrase as part of his justice-driven identity.
Kazuya immediately cuts through the comedy by attacking him.

cards.title: Costume 1: Street-Casual Training Wear
cards.summary: Bob's first costume is relaxed training wear based on Slim Bob's TEKKEN Tag Tournament 2 outfit, with Bob's Onigiri branding across the belly.
facts: The outfit represents Bob on a day off.
Words from his fighting style appear across the thighs, rear, gloves, and other details.
The bee motif returns from TEKKEN 6 and appears on Bob's skateboard.

cards.title: Costume 3: Investigator Formal Wear
cards.summary: Bob's formal costume reflects his role as an investigator for the Manhattan District Attorney's Office.
facts: The design shows the outfit Bob wears while on duty.
It gives Bob a formal look rarely used for the character before.
The costume connects his fighting role with his TEKKEN 8 legal mission.

faq.title: TEKKEN 8 Bob
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Bob's TEKKEN 8 release, moves, and Season 3 role

questions.question: When does Bob release in TEKKEN 8?
questions.answer: Bob is the second playable DLC character in TEKKEN 8 Season 3. Season Pass 3 holders get 5-day early access starting August 19, 2026 in North America, and Bob becomes available to all players on August 24-25, 2026 depending on region.

questions.question: Which platforms is TEKKEN 8 Bob available on?
questions.answer: TEKKEN 8 Bob is available on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and PC via Steam as part of TEKKEN 8 Season 3, either standalone or through the Season Pass 3.

questions.question: What is Bob's fighting style in TEKKEN 8?
questions.answer: TEKKEN 8 Bob uses Freestyle Karate with a Speed & Weight identity, blending large-frame power with agile close- to mid-range movement and stance-based offense.

questions.question: What is Spinner Ball in TEKKEN 8 Bob's moveset?
questions.answer: Spinner Ball is a returning stance in TEKKEN 8 Bob's kit that gains a new Heat Engager and expanded aerial combo options, including the Roulette Cutter follow-up.

questions.question: How does Bob's Heat Smash work in TEKKEN 8?
questions.answer: TEKKEN 8 Bob's Heat Smash features a just-frame input. Correct timing increases damage and triggers an alternate animation, rewarding precise execution.

questions.question: Is TEKKEN 8 Bob tournament-legal?
questions.answer: Yes. Under Tekken World Tour rules, TEKKEN 8 Bob becomes tournament-legal on his official release date, not during the early-access window.

questions.question: Who is Bob in TEKKEN 8's story?
questions.answer: In TEKKEN 8, Bob returns as the Onigiri Savior, a Freestyle Karate fighter and burger-chain founder who aids civilians during G Corp's invasion and opposes Kazuya Mishima as an emergency investigator.

questions.question: Do I need TEKKEN 8 to play Bob?
questions.answer: Yes. TEKKEN 8 Bob is DLC content that requires the TEKKEN 8 base game, available separately or through the Season 3 Pass on PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam.
